heading: Government policy and supporting position data
label: 3330f Government policy and supporting position data

L. 117–263section 101 of this title“(A)the quality of data required to be collected and whether the data is complete, accurate, timely, and reliable;“(B)any challenges experienced by agencies in implementing this subtitle and the amendments made by this subtitle; and“(C)any suggestions or modifications to enhance compliance with this subtitle and the amendments made by this subtitle, including best practices for agencies to follow.“(3).—Sunset of plum bookBeginning on —January 1, 2026“(A)the covered website shall serve as the public directory for policy and supporting positions in the Government; and“(B)the publication entitled ‘United States Government Policy and Supporting Positions’, commonly referred to as the ‘Plum Book’, shall no longer be issued or published.“(4)Funding.—“(A).—In generalNo additional amounts are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subtitle or the amendments made by this subtitle.“(B).—Other fundingThe Director shall carry out this subtitle and the amendments made by this subtitle using amounts otherwise available to the Director.”

children.label: A
children.text: any Executive agency, the United States Postal Service, and the Postal Regulatory Commission;

children.label: B
children.text: the Architect of the Capitol, the Government Accountability Office, the Government Publishing Office, and the Library of Congress; and

children.label: i
children.text: the Council of Economic Advisors;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Council on Environmental Quality;

children.label: iii
children.text: the National Security Council;

children.label: iv
children.text: the Office of the Vice President;

children.label: v
children.text: the Office of Policy Development;

children.label: vi
children.text: the Office of Administration;

children.label: vii
children.text: the Office of Management and Budget;

children.label: viii
children.text: the Office of the United States Trade Representative;

children.label: ix
children.text: the Office of Science and Technology Policy;

children.label: x
children.text: the Office of National Drug Control Policy; and

children.label: xi
children.text: the White House Office, including the White House Office of Presidential Personnel.

children.label: A
children.text: means an individual serving in a policy and supporting position; and

children.label: i
children.text: sections 3345 through 3349d (commonly referred to as the “Federal Vacancies Reform Act of 1998”);

children.label: ii
children.text: any other statutory provision described in section 3347(a)(1); or

children.label: iii
children.text: a Presidential appointment described in section 3347(a)(2).

children.heading: .—Covered website
children.label: 3 .—Covered website
children.text: The term “covered website” means the website established and maintained by the Director under subsection (b).

children.heading: .—Director
children.label: 4 .—Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of the Office of Personnel Management.

children.label: A
children.text: means any position at an agency, as determined by the Director, that, but for this section and section 2(b)(3) of the PLUM Act of 2022, would be included in the publication entitled “United States Government Policy and Supporting Positions”, (commonly referred to as the “Plum Book”); and

children.label: i
children.text: a position on any level of the Executive Schedule under subchapter II of chapter 53, or another position with an equivalent rate of pay;

children.label: ii
children.text: a general position (as defined in section 3132(a)(9)) in the Senior Executive service;

children.label: iii
children.text: a position in the Senior Foreign Service;

children.label: iv
children.text: a position of a confidential or policy-determining character under schedule C of subpart C of part 213 of title 5, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor regulation; and

children.label: v
children.text: any other position classified at or above level GS–14 of the General Schedule (or equivalent) that is excepted from the competitive service by law because of the confidential or policy-determining nature of the position duties.
Each policy and supporting position in the Federal Government, including any such position that is vacant.

children.label: A
children.text: is serving in a position described in paragraph (1); or

children.label: B
children.text: previously served in a position described in such paragraph under the applicable President.

children.label: A
children.text: any Government-wide or agency-wide limitation on the total number of positions in the Senior Executive Service under section 3133 or 3134 or the total number of positions under schedule C of subpart C of part 213 of title 5, Code of Federal Regulations; and

children.label: B
children.text: the total number of individuals occupying such positions.
the agency, and agency component, (including the agency and bureau code used by the Office of Management and Budget) in which the position is located;
the name of the position;
the name of the individual occupying the position (if any);
the geographic location of the position, including the city, State or province, and country;
the pay system under which the position is paid;
the level, grade, or rate of pay;
the term or duration of the appointment (if any);
the expiration date, in the case of a time-limited appointment;
a unique identifier for each appointee;
whether the position is vacant; and

children.label: A
children.text: for a position for which appointment is required to be made by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, the name of the acting official; and

children.label: B
children.text: for other positions, the name of the official performing the duties of the vacant position.

children.heading: .—Current Data
children.label: d .—Current Data
children.text: For each agency, the Director shall indicate in the information on the covered website the date that the agency last updated the data.

children.heading: .—Format
children.label: e .—Format
children.text: The Director shall make the data on the covered website available to the public at no cost over the internet in a searchable, sortable, downloadable, and machine-readable format so that the data qualifies as an open Government data asset, as defined in .section 3502 of title 44

children.heading: .—Information required
children.label: 1 .—Information required
children.text: Each agency shall provide to the Director any information that the Director determines necessary to establish and maintain the covered website, including the information uploaded under paragraph (4).

children.label: A
children.text: specific data standards that an agency shall follow to ensure that the information is complete, accurate, and reliable;

children.label: B
children.text: data quality assurance methods; and

children.label: C
children.text: the timeframe during which an agency shall provide or upload the information, including the timeframe described under paragraph (4).

children.label: A
children.text: the information required by the Director;

children.label: B
children.text: complete, accurate, and reliable information; or

children.label: C
children.text: the information during the timeframe specified by the Director.

children.label: i
children.text: the policy and supporting positions in the agency;

children.label: ii
children.text: the appointees occupying such positions in the agency; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the former appointees who served in such positions in the agency under the President then in office.

children.heading: .—Supplement not supplant
children.label: B .—Supplement not supplant
children.text: Information provided under subparagraph (A) shall supplement, not supplant, previously provided information under that subparagraph.

children.heading: .—Opm help desk
children.label: 5 .—Opm help desk
children.text: The Director shall establish a central help desk, to be operated by not more than 1 full-time employee, to assist any agency with implementing this section.

children.heading: .—Coordination
children.label: 6 .—Coordination
children.text: The Director may designate 1 or more agencies to participate in the development, establishment, operation, and support of the covered website. With respect to any such designation, the Director may specify the scope of the responsibilities of the agency so designated.

children.heading: .—Data standards and timing
children.label: 7 .—Data standards and timing
children.text: The Director shall make available on the covered website information regarding data collection standards, quality assurance methods, and time frames for reporting data to the Director.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: 8 .—Regulations
children.text: The Director may prescribe regulations necessary for the administration of this section.

children.heading: .—Provision of information
children.label: 1 .—Provision of information
children.text: Each agency shall comply with the instructions and guidance issued by the Director to carry out this section, and, upon request of the Director, shall provide appropriate assistance to the Director to ensure the successful operation of the covered website in the manner and within the timeframe specified by the Director under subsection (f)(2).

children.label: A
children.text: an explanation of how the agency ensured the information is complete, accurate, and reliable; and

children.label: B
children.text: a certification that the information is complete, accurate, and reliable.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: On the date that is 90 days after the date on which the covered website is established, the Director, in coordination with the White House Office of Presidential Personnel, shall confirm that the information on the covered website is complete, accurate, reliable, and up-to-date.

children.heading: .—Certification
children.label: B .—Certification
children.text: On the date on which the Director makes a confirmation under subparagraph (A), the Director shall publish on the covered website a certification that the confirmation has been made.

children.label: A
children.text: request additional information from an agency; and

children.label: B
children.text: use any additional information provided to the Director or the White House Office of Presidential Personnel for the purposes of verification.

children.heading: .—Public comment
children.label: 3 .—Public comment
children.text: The Director shall establish a process under which members of the public may provide feedback regarding the accuracy of the information on the covered website.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: As soon as practicable after a transitional inauguration day (as defined in section 3349a), the Director, in consultation with the Archivist of the United States, shall archive the data that was compiled on the covered website for the preceding presidential administration.

children.label: A
children.text: on, or through a link on, the covered website;

children.label: B
children.text: at no cost; and

children.label: C
children.text: in a searchable, sortable, downloadable, and machine-readable format.
